mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, which boosts consumer spending demand and drives immediate positive equity returns as earnings revisions turn upward.
evidence_refs: fact:2017 unemployment trend declining toward 4.4 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and rising retail sales indicate broad-based economic acceleration that validates corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns only when both indicators expand concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of co-occurring expansion episodes over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average signals supply-side capacity utilization reaching optimal levels, which compresses input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month average, or produce negative returns in over 40 percent of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence in near-term economic conditions, which translates into immediate positive momentum for broad equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no significant positive skew when HOUST posts sequential monthly increases across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 1.0 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to pressure high-multiple growth stocks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls confirm structural labor market tightening, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50 percent of periods where UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerating below 0.2 percent month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand, which rapidly compresses near-term earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2 percent
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are not significantly negative when RSAFS growth drops below 0.2 percent, or positive returns exceed 45 percent of triggered instances.
